replication_study.hypothesis: Mean aversiveness ratings for Covid-19 verbal jokes will be higher than for non-Covid verbal jokes; operationally, E[Aversiveness | label=covid-verbal] - E[Aversiveness | label=non-verbal] > 0 on the 0–6 scale.

data_plan.dataset_identifier: Bischetti Survey Replication (Doomlab) — deidentified Part 1 and Part 2 (Jan 2021)
data_plan.source_type: Online survey (Prolific) of participants rating internet jokes for aversiveness/funniness
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Combined Part 1 and Part 2 after filtering test sessions using created timestamps: > 2021-01-19 17:52:38 (Part 1) and > 2021-01-19 17:55:15 (Part 2), per Analysis_updated.Rmd.
data_plan.sample_size: To be computed at runtime after cleaning and merging; before filtering there are 1336 unique participant_id in Part 1 and 1051 in Part 2 (deidentified CSVs). Expected final N ≈ 1000–1300 unique participants with multiple item ratings.
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Participant-by-item rating (aversiveness per item per participant).
data_plan.access_details: Deidentified CSVs are available in /app/data as Bischetti_Survey_Part1_deidentify.csv and Bischetti_Survey_Part2_deidentify.csv. An item-to-label mapping file pic_label_map.csv (columns: name,label) is REQUIRED in /app/data to run the focal contrast; a template can be generated by make_label_template__py.py.

qualification.explanation: This dataset is well-suited as a direct-sample replication: it captures the same constructs (aversiveness ratings of Covid-related vs non-Covid humor items) using an online survey format with comparable item types. It provides an independent sample collected under similar conditions, thus enabling a robust test of the original phenomenon on new data.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Original study: Large-scale online survey of participants rating internet jokes (Covid Verbal, Covid Meme, Covid Strip, and Non-Covid Verbal) on funniness and aversiveness (0–6 scale after re-coding), analyzed with linear mixed-effects models with random effects for participants, items, and province (see post_registration.json: data/method summaries; original_paper.pdf summary: humor types, dependent variables, and modeling approach). Replication data: Contains the same aversiveness ratings by item (columns like pic*_disturbing) and participant identifiers, with additional CHS/LOTR scales (see replication_data CSV columns and Analysis_updated.Rmd). The updated Rmd explicitly re-codes Aversiveness to 0–6 and models random effects (participants, items, and state), mirroring the original approach.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original sample: 1751 native Italian speakers, March 18–30, 2020, with province-level location (post_registration.json and original_paper.pdf). Replication sample: Prolific-based deidentified participants with a US-style 'state' variable (e.g., CO) and collection date filters in Jan 2021 (replication_data/Analysis_updated.Rmd shows state fix to 'CO' and timestamp filters). Geographic random effect is 'state' instead of Italian provinces, and the sample is distinct in time, language/context, and recruitment platform. Additionally, the replication package lacks the original Method Excel files (Bischetti_Survey_Part1.xlsx/Part2.xlsx) that map items to humor-type labels; a new mapping must be provided (or constructed) for this dataset.

data_plan.notes: Critical missing metadata: item-to-label mapping required to categorize each pic* item to 'covid-verbal', 'covid-meme', 'covid-strip', or 'non-verbal'. The Rmds merge on Method/Bischetti_Survey_Part1.xlsx and Part2.xlsx to obtain 'label'; these files are not provided. The Python pipeline expects /app/data/pic_label_map.csv (name,label). A helper script (make_label_template__py.py) can generate a template from the CSV headers. Aversiveness scale recoding: the updated Rmd subtracts 1 to convert 1–7 to 0–6; the Python script mirrors this. Age appears to be year-of-birth, recoded to age years (2021 - age). Empty state values are set to UNK for random effect grouping.

steps: Run make_label_template__py.py to produce /app/data/pic_label_map_template.csv and complete it with labels for all items appearing in your study, saving as /app/data/pic_label_map.csv (columns: name,label; label in {covid-verbal, covid-meme, covid-strip, non-verbal}). At minimum, label items that are covid-verbal and non-verbal for the focal test.

Execute Analysis_updated__py.py. It will: (a) filter test sessions using timestamps from the Rmd, (b) remove known duplicate/refreshed sessions by session id lists, (c) merge Part 1 and Part 2 on participant_id, (d) reshape to long format, (e) recode aversiveness to 0–6, (f) merge item labels from pic_label_map.csv, (g) fit a mixed-effects model, and (h) write outputs.

Inspect /app/data/replication_results.json for model summary and the focal contrast (non-verbal minus covid-verbal difference, its test statistic, and p-value). Confirm descriptive means for covid-verbal and non-verbal also align with the focal claim structure.
planned_method.models: Linear mixed-effects model using statsmodels MixedLM: Aversiveness ~ label + (1|participant_id) + (1|name) + (1|state) via variance components (groups: participant_id; vc: item and state). Treatment coding with baseline 'covid-verbal'; the coefficient for label[T.non-verbal] estimates (non-verbal - covid-verbal).
planned_method.independent_variables: Primary: label (humor type), focusing on 'covid-verbal' vs 'non-verbal' for the focal replication.
planned_method.control_variables: None in the fixed effects for the focal test; random intercepts for participant, item, and state capture clustering. Age slope is omitted due to convergence issues in the updated Rmd.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Fixed-effect contrast of label levels: coefficient for label[T.non-verbal] under treatment-coding baseline 'covid-verbal' (i.e., difference = mean(non-verbal) - mean(covid-verbal)).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Wald z-test (from MixedLM) for the label[T.non-verbal] coefficient; two-sided. Expect a negative coefficient (~ -0.39) corresponding to original covid-verbal minus non-verbal difference of +0.39.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion for missing Aversiveness; empty state values encoded as 'UNK' for random-effects grouping.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not applicable for the focal replication contrast; no correction planned.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-sided p < 0.05 for the label contrast; direction should be negative for (non-verbal - covid-verbal), equivalent to positive for (covid-verbal - non-verbal).

files.Analysis_updated__py.py: Main Python replication script. Reads /app/data/Bischetti_Survey_Part1_deidentify.csv and /app/data/Bischetti_Survey_Part2_deidentify.csv; filters timestamps and removes duplicate sessions (per Rmd lists), merges halves on participant_id, reshapes to long, recodes Aversiveness (0–6), merges labels from /app/data/pic_label_map.csv (name,label), fits a mixed-effects model (Aversiveness ~ label + random effects for participant_id, item, state), and writes outputs to /app/data (replication_results.json and aversiveness_long.csv).
files.make_label_template__py.py: Helper script to generate /app/data/pic_label_map_template.csv listing all item 'name' identifiers inferred from the deidentified CSV headers (pic*_disturbing -> pic*_anote). Fill the 'label' column with one of: covid-verbal, covid-meme, covid-strip, non-verbal, and save the completed file as /app/data/pic_label_map.csv for use by Analysis_updated__py.py.

codebase.notes: All IO is under /app/data. The R Markdown files (Analysis.Rmd, Analysis_updated.Rmd) are retained for reference but are not executed. Entry point for replication is Analysis_updated__py.py. A required external input is /app/data/pic_label_map.csv, which replaces the missing Method Excel mapping from the Rmd. Dependencies are listed under docker_specs. The MixedLM model uses participant_id as the grouping factor and variance components for item and state.

instructions: Ensure the following files are present in ./data (mounted to /app/data): Bischetti_Survey_Part1_deidentify.csv, Bischetti_Survey_Part2_deidentify.csv.

Run: python /app/data/../original/21/input/replication_data/make_label_template__py.py to create /app/data/pic_label_map_template.csv. Fill in labels and save as /app/data/pic_label_map.csv (columns: name,label).
Review outputs: /app/data/replication_results.json (model summary; fixed effects; focal contrast stats) and /app/data/aversiveness_long.csv (analysis-ready data).
comparison_metrics: Direction of effect: Covid verbal aversiveness higher than Non-Covid verbal (expect negative coefficient for non-verbal vs covid-verbal in our coding).
Magnitude: Compare estimated difference to original +0.39 (Covid verbal minus Non-Covid verbal). In our output, difference is non-verbal minus covid-verbal, so expect approximately -0.39.
Statistical significance: Compare p-value to original p<.05; optionally compare test statistic magnitude (original t=+2.26).
Descriptive means: Compare estimated means for covid-verbal and non-verbal categories to original M=0.87 and M=0.48.
